stories.title: Welcome to Our School
sentences: Welcome to our school.
It is big and beautiful.
Look, this is the teachers' office.
It is on the first floor.
The library is next to the teachers' office.
Do you have an art room?
Yes, we do.
It is on the second floor.
We like our school very much.

questions.question: The teachers' office is on the second floor.
questions.explanation: The story says the teachers' office is on the first floor.

questions.question: Where is the library?
options: It is next to the art room.
It is next to the teachers' office.
questions.answer: It is next to the teachers' office.
questions.explanation: The sentence states: 'The library is next to the teachers' office.'

questions.question: The art room is on the ________ floor.
questions.explanation: The story says the art room is on the second floor.

stories.title: John's Busy Day
sentences: Today is Monday.
John is at the playground.
He plays with his sister.
Where is the computer room?
Is it next to the library?
No, it isn't.
It is on the second floor.
It is time for computer class.
John runs to the room.

questions.question: John is with his sister at the playground.
questions.explanation: The story says 'He plays with his sister' at the playground.

questions.question: What class does John have?
questions.explanation: The story mentions 'It is time for computer class.'

questions.question: The computer room is on the ________ floor.
questions.explanation: The text states the computer room is on the second floor.

stories.title: A Busy Morning for Zip
sentences: Wake up, Zip! It's 7:00 a.m. now.
It's time to get up and have breakfast.
The milk is hot and the bread is good.
What time is it now? It is 7:30 a.m.
It's time for school. Zip goes to school by bike.
At 8:00, it's time for English class.
Zip reads a book and speaks English with Zoom.
They are very happy at school today.

questions.question: Zip gets up at 7:00 a.m.
questions.explanation: The story says 'It's 7:00 a.m. now. It's time to get up'.

questions.question: What does Zip do at 8:00?
questions.explanation: The text states 'At 8:00, it's time for English class'.

questions.question: Zip goes to school by ______.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Zip goes to school by bike'.

stories.title: Mike's Late Lunch
sentences: Mike is at home on Saturday morning.
He plays with his toy car in the room.
What time is it? It is 12:00 o'clock.
Oh! It is time for lunch.
But Mike is not hungry. He wants to play.
At 3:00 p.m., Mike feels very hungry.
Now it is time to eat some noodles.
He eats and drinks some orange juice.
Time to go to the playground now!

questions.question: It is time for lunch at 12:00 o'clock.
questions.explanation: The story says at 12:00 it is time for lunch.

questions.question: What is Mike doing at 12:00?
options: He is playing with a toy car.
questions.answer: He is playing with a toy car.
questions.explanation: The story says he plays with his toy car and then checks the time at 12:00.

questions.question: At 3:00 p.m., Mike eats some ______.
questions.explanation: The text states 'Now it is time to eat some noodles'.

stories.title: Sam's Dilemma
sentences: It is seven o'clock in the morning.
Sam looks at the window.
It is cloudy and windy today.
"Mum, can I go outside?" Sam asks.
"No, you can't," Mum says.
"Wait, look! Now it is rainy."
Sam is sad.
He can't play football in the park.
He stays home and reads a book.

questions.question: The weather is sunny in the morning.
questions.explanation: The story says it is cloudy and windy, and later it becomes rainy.

questions.question: What is the weather like when Mum says 'no'?
questions.explanation: Mum points out that now it is rainy, so Sam cannot go outside.

questions.question: Sam wants to play ______ in the park.
questions.explanation: The story mentions Sam cannot play football due to the rain.

stories.title: A Phone Call to Grandpa
sentences: Chen Jie calls her grandpa in Harbin.
"Hi Grandpa! What's the weather like in Harbin?"
"It is snowy and very cold here," says Grandpa.
"Oh! In Shenzhen, it is sunny and warm."
Chen Jie wants to go outside and swim.
"Can I swim today, Mum?" she asks.
"Yes, you can," says her mum.
Chen Jie is very happy.

questions.question: Where is Grandpa now?
questions.explanation: The first sentence states Chen Jie calls her grandpa in Harbin.

questions.question: Chen Jie can go swimming today.
questions.explanation: Her mum says 'Yes, you can' because it is sunny and warm in Shenzhen.

questions.question: The weather in Harbin is ______ and very cold.
questions.explanation: Grandpa describes the weather in Harbin as snowy and cold.

stories.title: Old Macdonald's Magic Garden
sentences: Welcome to my big farm.
Look at these big red tomatoes.
They are so fresh.
What are those over there?
They are potatoes under the tall grass.
The potatoes are small and brown.
Are these apples or oranges?
No, they are big yellow carrots.
How many carrots do you have?
I have fifteen carrots today.

questions.question: The tomatoes on the farm are red.
questions.explanation: The story says 'Look at these big red tomatoes'.

questions.question: What color are the potatoes?
questions.explanation: The text states 'The potatoes are small and brown.'

questions.question: How many carrots are there on the farm today?
questions.explanation: The speaker says 'I have fifteen carrots today.'

stories.title: A Busy Day with Animals
sentences: It is a sunny day at the farm.
I see many animals near the park.
Look at these horses.
They are very tall and strong.
Are those sheep over there?
Yes, they are.
How many cows do you see?
I see twenty cows.
The cows like eating green grass.
The farm is so much fun.

questions.question: The narrator sees ten cows.
questions.explanation: The story says 'I see twenty cows', not ten.

questions.question: What do the cows like to do?
options: They like sleeping in the park.
questions.explanation: The text says 'The cows like eating green grass.'

questions.question: The horses are tall and ________.
questions.explanation: The story describes the horses as 'tall and strong'.

stories.title: A Busy Morning
sentences: It is seven o'clock.
Amy wants to go to the park.
She puts on her red dress.
But she cannot find her hat.
She sees a blue hat on the bed.
She asks her brother, "Is this yours?"
Sam says, "No, it is not mine."
It is Mum's hat.
Amy puts the blue hat on the desk.
Finally, she finds her yellow hat under the chair.

questions.question: Amy wants to go to school.
questions.explanation: The story says Amy wants to go to the park, not school.

questions.question: Whose hat is the blue one?
questions.explanation: The text states 'It is Mum's hat' when referring to the blue hat.

questions.question: Amy finds her yellow hat under the _______.
questions.explanation: The final sentence says she finds her hat under the chair.

stories.title: Clean Identity
sentences: The clothes are clean now.
Mike helps his mother hang up the clothes.
"Whose pants are these?" Mike asks.
They are very long.
They are his father's pants.
Then he sees some small socks.
"Are these yours, Sarah?" Mike asks his sister.
"Yes, they are mine," says Sarah.
Sarah likes her white socks.
Now the clothes are all on the line.

questions.question: The pants are very short.
questions.explanation: The story says the pants are very long because they are his father's.

questions.question: What is Mike doing?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Mike helps his mother hang up the clothes'.

questions.question: The socks are ________.
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'Sarah likes her white socks'.

stories.title: A New Hat for Little Rabbit
sentences: Little Rabbit goes to the clothes shop with her mother.
She sees a pretty hat on the table.
It is pink and it has a little apple on it.
"How much is this hat?" asks Little Rabbit.
"It is fifteen yuan," says the people in the shop.
That is very cheap!
Little Rabbit puts the hat on her head.
It is not too big or too small.
It is just right for the party.

questions.question: The pretty hat has a little apple on it.
questions.explanation: The story says 'it has a little apple on it'.

questions.question: How much is the hat?
questions.explanation: The text states the hat is fifteen yuan.

questions.question: The hat is not too big or too small, it is ________.
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'It is just right for the party'.

stories.title: Shopping for New Shoes
sentences: Zhang Peng wants to buy a pair of cool shoes.
He sees some blue shoes in the window.
"How much are those shoes?" asks Zhang Peng.
They are ninety-nine yuan.
"Oh, they are too expensive!" says Zhang Peng.
Then he finds some black shoes on a small table.
They are only thirty yuan.
They are cheap and they look nice.
The people in the shop help him try them on.

questions.question: The blue shoes are very cheap.
questions.explanation: The blue shoes are ninety-nine yuan and Zhang Peng says they are too expensive.

questions.question: Where does Zhang Peng find the black shoes?
questions.explanation: The story says he finds some black shoes on a small table.

questions.question: Zhang Peng wants to buy a pair of ________.
questions.explanation: The first sentence says he wants to buy a pair of cool shoes.
